data.title: Abu Dhabi sovereign fund quintuples Bitcoin investment
data.description:

data.title: Planet Evening News | November 21
data.description: 1. MSCI is highly likely to remove "Digital Asset Reserve Companies," which will put enormous pressure on related companies; 2. Tom Lee maintains a bullish view, predicting Bitcoin will rise to $200,000 by the end of January next year; 3. A whale deposited $5 million USDC into HyperLiquid for the first time and went long on ETH with 20x leverage; 4. Binance Futures launched BOBUSDT perpetual contracts with up to 20x leverage; 5. Top 3 DAT companies: Strategy earned $6.15 billion, Bitmine lost $4.52 billion, and Forward lost $711 million; 6. Analysts: Bitcoin may form a "final bottom" in the $73,000 to $84,000 range; 7. Andrew Tate was liquidated again for going long on BTC, bringing his total liquidations on HyperLiquid to 84; 8. Strategy's Bitcoin holdings fell below $55 billion, with unrealized profits dropping sharply to approximately $6 billion; 9. DWF Head: A new fund of $30 million to $75 million will be launched, focusing on DeFi/CeDeFi products; 10. Opinion: STRK's market performance today is similar to ZEC on October 11; 11. A hardcore short whale's 20x leveraged Bitcoin short position now has a floating profit of over $34 million; 12. The probability of "SOL falling to $100 in November" on Polymarket has risen to 21%; 13. BTC plunged to $82,000, ETH fell to $2,669, and five Hyperliquid accounts were liquidated, resulting in a loss of over $10 million; 14. Machi's 25x leveraged ETH long position was liquidated, leaving his wallet with only $15,538, a single loss of $1.05 million; 15. The US Treasury Secretary appeared at the opening ceremony of a Bitcoin-themed bar, which the Bitcoin community sees as a positive sign.

data.title: Hong Kong Financial Services and Treasury Bureau: Will promote the tokenization of the bond market; details to be announced in the first half of 2026.
data.description: Hong Kong's Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Christopher Hui, stated that they are jointly advancing a study on the applicability of existing legislation to tokenized bonds, aiming to promote the adoption of tokenization technology in Hong Kong's bond market. Details will be announced in the first half of next year. Hui mentioned that the previously issued third batch of tokenized green bonds introduced the option of settlement in RMB and HKD tokenized central bank currencies, making them among the world's first digital bonds to utilize these two tokenized central bank currencies in the settlement process. (Hong Kong Government Press Release)

$2 billion vanishes in crypto liquidations as Bitcoin plunges to $82,000 low.

data.title: AI Stocks and European Markets Experience Decline
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, several AI-related stocks in the U.S. market saw a decline in pre-market trading. Nvidia's shares fell by 3%, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company dropped by 2.6%, AMD decreased by 2.8%, and Oracle's shares were down by 2.3%.

The European STOXX 600 Index also experienced a downturn, currently down by 1.1%. The European Technology Index fell by 3.1%, reaching its lowest point since mid-September. Additionally, the European market volatility index rose to its highest level since May.

In the commodities market, WTI crude oil dropped by 2.00%, currently priced at $57.51 per barrel, while Brent crude oil decreased by 1.78%. Spot silver fell below $49 per ounce for the first time since November 10, with a daily decline of 3.29%.

data.title: Why Is Crypto Down Today? – November 21, 2025
data.description:
The crypto market is down today. BTC fell 8. 7% to $84,152, and ETH dropped 10% to $2,729. Macroeconomic jitters may turn into full-blown panic and the sell-off intensify.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/why-is-crypto-down-today-november-21-2025/

data.title: Glassnode: Bitcoin's actual losses have surged to their highest level since the FTX crash.
data.description: Glassnode stated on its X platform that Bitcoin realized losses have surged to the highest level since the FTX crash, with short-term holders being the main driver of this sell-off. The scale and speed of liquidations during the decline reflect a significant decrease in marginal demand, with recent new buyers closing out their positions.

data.title: Bitcoin's Recovery May Be Delayed Amid Weak Market Conditions
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, shared insights on the X platform, highlighting that the market's weakness has exceeded expectations. He suggested that if these conditions persist, Bitcoin might not experience a strong rebound in the next 3-6 months. A genuine bull market is anticipated to commence next year when liquidity is restored.

Ki Young Ju also referenced macroeconomic expert Luke Gromen, noting the weak demand for U.S. Treasury bonds from foreign entities. Without new liquidity injections, the U.S. Treasury market could face instability. Once liquidity is restored next year, the prices of scarce assets like gold and Bitcoin are expected to rise.

Earlier today, Ki Young Ju advised against selling or shorting spot holdings, deeming such actions unwise at this time.

data.description: According to on-chain data, the highest price of KAIA in the 24-hour period of the 26th Gate Contract Points airdrop was $0.08570, with a current market capitalization of approximately $440 million. The single-account profit for this airdrop was nearly $33. It is understood that this airdrop was divided into two prize pools: users holding ≥ 40 Gate Contract Points could receive a 100 USDT position trial voucher, consuming 20 Gate Contract Points; those holding ≥ 120 Gate Contract Points could receive 380 KAIA, also consuming 20 Gate Contract Points. The 27th Gate Contract Points airdrop, offering 50 GRASS and a 100 USDT position trial voucher per person, will begin on November 24th at 19:00 (UTC+8). Join now for a chance to claim rewards tomorrow.

data.title: FLock's AI Model Launchpad Proposal Sparks Interest
data.description:
According to PANews, FLock's annual performance report has introduced an intriguing concept of an AI Model Launchpad. This initiative aims to distribute assets to trained AI models, driven by infrastructure layers, across various specialized scenarios such as medical diagnostics, legal documentation, financial risk management, and supply chain optimization.

The report highlights the challenges faced by these specialized models, which have limited commercialization paths, often leading to acquisition by large companies or open-sourcing without sustainable monetization. FLock proposes using tokenomics to restructure this value chain, allowing contributors to model training, such as data providers, computing nodes, and validators, to potentially earn long-term revenue rights. Revenue generated from model usage would be distributed proportionally based on contributions.

The concept of an AI Model Launchpad is novel, utilizing financial mechanisms to drive product development. Asset tokenization of models provides trainers with motivation for continuous optimization and fosters a self-sustaining ecosystem.

The benefits of this approach are evident, as seen in the recent popularity of the nof1 AI model trading competition, which currently features general models due to the lack of incentives for specialized models. Asset-backed models could transform such competitions into platforms for showcasing capabilities, with performance directly impacting asset value.

While FLock's proposal is still in its conceptual stage, the specifics of asset distribution for models and agents remain unclear. Ensuring genuine demand for asset-backed models and addressing challenges in specialized scenarios are critical issues that need resolution.

The anticipation surrounding FLock's AI Model Launchpad is palpable, with expectations for innovative approaches in this direction.

data.title: Binance to Launch Mastercard Deposits and Withdrawals for Kazakhstani Tenge
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce Mastercard deposits and withdrawals for the Kazakhstani Tenge (KZT). This new feature will allow users from Binance Kazakhstan to make instant KZT deposits and purchase a wide range of cryptocurrencies using their KZT balance. The initiative aims to enhance the user experience by simplifying the process of buying cryptocurrencies with local currency.

To mark the launch, Binance is organizing two exclusive promotions. Promotion A offers users the chance to win an 8YA Merchandise Box and USDT token vouchers. Eligible participants must confirm their participation on the activity page and achieve a cumulative deposit of at least 250,000 KZT during the promotion period, which runs from 2025-11-21 05:00 (UTC) to 2025-12-21 18:59 (UTC). Deposits made via Mastercard issued by any Kazakhstani banks or through fiat partners like FreedomBank and Kazpost are included in the promotion. The prize pool is structured to reward the top depositors, with the first-place winner receiving an 8YA Merchandise Box and a 250 USDT token voucher.

Promotion B targets users who have never made a fiat deposit, withdrawal, or used the Buy/Sell Crypto services with fiat. These users can receive a $5 bonus in the form of a deposit fee voucher, available on a first-come, first-served basis to the first 900 eligible users. Additionally, users who deposit and buy any cryptocurrency with at least $50 using their KZT balance stand a chance to win an iPhone 17 and AirPods. The rewards for these promotions will be distributed within three weeks after the promotion ends, with winners receiving their prizes in the form of USDT token vouchers. Binance emphasizes that only verified users of Binance Kazakhstan are eligible for these promotions.

data.title: Tom Lee Predicts Bitcoin Surge Despite Market Downturn
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, despite the recent downturn in the cryptocurrency market, Tom Lee remains optimistic about Bitcoin's future. In an interview, Lee predicted that Bitcoin could reach between $150,000 and $200,000 by the end of January next year. He attributes this potential rise to the enduring influence of retail investors in driving the market upward. Lee emphasized that those who have not maintained a bullish outlook over the past decade have consistently been proven wrong.

Notably, Lee made a similar prediction during the 2025 Korea Blockchain Week Impact Summit in September, forecasting that Bitcoin could reach $200,000 to $250,000 by the end of the year, with Ethereum's target set at $10,000 to $12,000.
